hypothesis: Incorporating a dynamic, real-time adjustment of the bicarbonate-to-carbonate ratio during electrolysis—guided by continuous feedback from in-line product sensing and an online Bayesian optimizer—will sustain reactor yield above 44 mA cm⁻² for extended operation by compensating for the time-dependent depletion of bicarbonate and accumulation of carbonate that otherwise shifts the system away from the optimal 1.5 M KHCO₃ / 0.75 M K₂CO₃ composition identified under static conditions.
